creator: vgrebenschikov@gmail.com
text:
Got the similar problem with alc driver.

It worked fine with usual configuration, but when I turn on 802.1Q on the port and pass traffic through VLAN, it starts to work really slow,

It has relation to only traffic originated on node, traffic that passed through gateway (from one vlan to another) goes with normal speed.

pf is not used et all, only ipfw.

by configuration:

gateway_enable="YES"
ifconfig_alc0="up"
vlans_alc0="vlan1 vlan2"
create_args_vlan1="vlan 1"
create_args_vlan2="vlan 2"
ifconfig_vlan1="inet 192.168.2.5/24"
ifconfig_vlan1_alias0="192.168.1.5/24"
ifconfig_vlan2="inet 77.23.11.68/26"

TCP traffic from 192.168.2.5 on vlan1 stalls (very slow).
ICMP pings - are fine.

FreeBSD srv 9.3-STABLE FreeBSD 9.3-STABLE #1 r271608: Mon Sep 15 11:06:18 MSK 2014     root@srv:/usr/obj/usr/src/sys/SRV  amd64

alc0: <Atheros AR8131 PCIe Gigabit Ethernet> port 0xec00-0xec7f mem 0xfebc0000-0xfebfffff irq 18 at device 0.0 on pci2
alc0: 15872 Tx FIFO, 15360 Rx FIFO
alc0: Using 1 MSI message(s).
alc0: 4GB boundary crossed, switching to 32bit DMA addressing mode.
miibus0: <MII bus> on alc0
atphy0: <Atheros F1 10/100/1000 PHY> PHY 0 on miibus0
atphy0:  none, 10baseT, 10baseT-FDX, 100baseTX, 100baseTX-FDX, 1000baseT-FDX, 1000baseT-FDX-master, auto, auto-flow
alc0: Ethernet address: bc:ae:cc:55:bb:3f


when I just turn off tagging for vlan1 and assign IP address on alc0 directly it works fine again on local network. (did not check outgoing traffic from vlan2 to external world yet).
